message.content: {"items":[{"title":"Federal Republic of Germany 1969: Post-Election Survey (German: Bundestagswahl 1969 – Nachwahluntersuchung) – with items on family planning and attitudes toward abortion","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.7729","justification":"Part of the German National Election Studies; contains modules on social policy, family planning, and abortion attitudes in the Federal Republic of Germany at the end of the 1960s/early 1970s, relevant for reproductive health context."},{"title":"Population Policy and Birth Control in the Federal Republic of Germany 1970 (German: Bevölkerungspolitik und Geburtenregelung in der Bundesrepublik Deutschland 1970)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2574","justification":"Survey focuses specifically on population policy, contraception, and birth control in West Germany around 1970, directly linked to reproductive health in that period."},{"title":"Family Formation and Family Planning in the Federal Republic of Germany 1971","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2575","justification":"Dataset addresses family formation, desired number of children, contraceptive practices, and related attitudes in West Germany in 1971, central to reproductive health behavior."},{"title":"Family Planning and Contraception in the Federal Republic of Germany 1972","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2576","justification":"Survey focuses on knowledge and use of contraceptive methods, sources of information, and attitudes towards family planning in 1972 West Germany."},{"title":"Attitudes toward Abortion and Family Planning, Federal Republic of Germany 1973","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2577","justification":"Concentrates on public opinion about abortion law, indications for legal abortion, and access to family planning services in early 1970s West Germany."},{"title":"Youth and Sexuality in the Federal Republic of Germany 1970 (German: Jugend und Sexualität 1970)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2569","justification":"Examines sexual behavior, contraception, and knowledge of reproductive health among young people in West Germany around 1970."},{"title":"Youth and Sexuality in the Federal Republic of Germany 1978","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2570","justification":"Late‑1970s follow‑up on youth sexual behavior, contraceptive use, and reproductive attitudes, still within the requested decade and German context."},{"title":"Women and Family Planning in the Federal Republic of Germany 1976","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.2578","justification":"Focuses on women’s contraceptive practices, desired fertility, and attitudes toward pregnancy and abortion in mid‑1970s West Germany."},{"title":"Microcensus Scientific Use Files (West Germany 1970s) – fertility and family supplements","url_or_doi":"https://www.gesis.org/en/microdata/microcensus","justification":"Microcensus SUFs for the 1970s include fertility, number of children, marriage, and family planning–related variables for West Germany, providing large‑scale reproductive health context."},{"title":"German Family and Fertility Survey – Retrospective data covering cohorts whose reproductive years include the 1970s","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4872","justification":"Though fielded later, it contains retrospective life‑course data on fertility, contraception, and partnership histories for Germany, allowing analysis of reproductive behavior in the 1970s."}]}
